task_completion.description: Open the input FASTA file containing the cytochrome c sequences.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bioinformatics software GUI (e.g., UGENE) is open and displays the loaded file 'cytochrome_c_multispecies.fasta' with the sequences for the 8 species visible.

task_completion.description: Select and configure the MUSCLE algorithm for multiple sequence alignment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu, dialog box, or settings panel is visible showing 'MUSCLE' selected as the alignment method.
An alignment viewer is visible showing the sequences aligned together, typically indicated by colored amino acid blocks and introduced gaps (dashes) across the sequences.

task_completion.description: Generate the phylogenetic tree using the built-in tree builder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A phylogenetic tree viewer or window is open, displaying a graphical tree (dendrogram) that connects the 8 species or their corresponding accessions.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting tree in Newick format to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog shows the file being saved to '~/UGENE_Data/results/cytochrome_c_tree.nwk' with the Newick format selected, or a file manager/terminal confirms the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used the software GUI to perform the alignment and tree building.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the graphical interface of the bioinformatics software (e.g., UGENE) rather than executing command-line scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type or paste the Newick tree string into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) being used to manually create or paste content into 'cytochrome_c_tree.nwk'.

integrity.description: The generated tree corresponds to the actual input data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The phylogenetic tree displayed in the software must actually contain the 8 species mentioned in the prompt (or their UniProt accessions), proving it was generated from the provided FASTA file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input FASTA file contains UniProt cytochrome c sequences with accessions P99999, P67881, P00048, P00053, P00044, P04657, P00004, P62895. The expected output file must be saved exactly at ~/UGENE_Data/results/cytochrome_c_tree.nwk.
